feed: Nevada County Experience — Curated Venues & Experiences

venues.name: The Curious Forge
venues.blurb: Blacksmithing, ceramics, glass & making workshops
venues.notes: 20,000 sq ft makerspace; metalwork, ceramics, glass, woodworking, CNC

venues.name: The Center for the Arts
venues.blurb: Concerts, performances, gallery, classes
venues.hours: Box office Mon–Fri 1:30–5:30 PM; event times vary
venues.notes: Major county arts anchor; hosts Music in the Mountains, Sierra Poetry Festival

venues.name: Miners Foundry Cultural Center
venues.blurb: Live music, performances, community events
venues.hours: Event times vary; box office Tue–Fri 10–3:30
venues.notes: Historic 1856 foundry; Wild & Scenic Film Festival venue

venues.name: First Friday Art Walk – Nevada City
venues.blurb: Art walk with music, performances & vendors
venues.notes: Downtown-wide activation; free & walkable

venues.name: The Louvre Framing, Restoration & Gallery
venues.blurb: Downtown Grass Valley gallery with framing & art restoration
venues.notes: 124 E Main St — rotating local-artist exhibits alongside custom framing and fine-art restoration

venues.name: The Gallery at Grass Valley Courtyard Suites
venues.blurb: Rotating local-art exhibits in a boutique-inn gallery space
venues.hours: Open during inn hours; check gvcourtyardsuites.com
venues.notes: 210 S Auburn St — gallery space inside Grass Valley Courtyard Suites showcasing rotating exhibitions of local artists

venues.name: Friar Tuck's Restaurant & Bar
venues.blurb: Landmark restaurant & bar with live music every night
venues.hours: Dinner nightly; live music nightly — Fri/Sat two acts (4:30 & 7 PM)
venues.notes: 111 N Pine St — Nevada City's beloved table since 1973 in an 1860 brick building; fondue, steaks & seafood; live music every night of the week; (530) 265-9093

venues.name: Elixart
venues.blurb: All-ages herbal elixir lounge, art space & live-music venue
venues.hours: Wed–Sun 11 AM–7 PM; closed Mon & Tue; evening events vary
venues.notes: 408 Broad St — alcohol-free elixir & kava bar in the Historic District; rotating local art on the walls and frequent live music & community events; (530) 265-1901

venues.name: ArtWorks Gallery
venues.blurb: 35+ local artists in a 19th-century Grass Valley building
venues.notes: Part of California Cultural District; 2,000 sq ft of local art

venues.name: Hardy Books
venues.blurb: Rare & out-of-print books on all things Californian
venues.notes: One-of-a-kind California history titles & local authors; literary treasure trove

venues.name: Empire Mine State Historic Park
venues.blurb: Mineyard tours + blacksmith shop viewing
venues.notes: Richest hard-rock gold mine in CA; 5.8M oz over 106 years

venues.name: Nevada County Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum
venues.blurb: Museum tours, railbus rides, Steam-Up Sunday
venues.notes: Engine 5 starred in John Waynes The Spoilers; strong heritage add-on

venues.name: Nevada County Historical Society
venues.blurb: Walking tours + Firehouse Museum
venues.notes: Self-guided downtown history layer; 1861 firehouse building

venues.name: Grass Valley Museum and Cultural Center
venues.blurb: Museum in St. Josephs Cultural Center
venues.notes: Hear the story of Lola Montez exotic bathtub; easy add-on near downtown

venues.name: North Star Mining Museum & Pelton Wheel
venues.blurb: Worlds largest Pelton Wheel exhibit
venues.notes: 30-ft Pelton Wheel laid foundation for modern hydroelectric power; free admission

venues.name: Firehouse No. 1 Museum
venues.blurb: Victorian-era firehouse with Gold Country artifacts
venues.notes: Houses the Hou Wang Temple altar from Grass Valley Chinatown; free entry

venues.name: Nevada Theatre
venues.blurb: Oldest continuously operated theater in California (1865)
venues.hours: Check event calendar; box office varies
venues.notes: Built 1865; stunning murals by Sarah Coleman; hosts Wild & Scenic, film festivals

venues.name: Nevada City Smartphone Walking Tour
venues.blurb: 90-minute self-guided historic downtown audio tour
venues.notes: Covers architecture & stories from 1850–1970s; free or low-cost

venues.name: Crazy Horse Saloon & Grill
venues.blurb: Live music + dining
venues.notes: Live bands most weekends; good evening stop

venues.name: Music in the Mountains
venues.blurb: Annual classical music festival
venues.notes: World-class classical & chamber music; community tradition since 1982

venues.name: Wild & Scenic Film Festival
venues.blurb: Annual environmental & adventure film festival (SYRCL)
venues.notes: 110+ films; one of nations largest env. film festivals; multiple venues

venues.name: Nevada City Film Festival
venues.blurb: "Sundance of the Sierra" — independent film festival
venues.hours: Annual — typically June/July; outdoor screenings at sunset
venues.notes: USA Today #3 Best Film Festival; outdoor screenings with lawn games, beer & wine

venues.name: Restaurant DaKan
venues.blurb: Farm-to-table dining
venues.notes: Local ingredient-driven; creative seasonal menu

venues.name: Petra
venues.blurb: Mediterranean farm-to-table
venues.notes: Women-owned; locally sourced; organic emphasis

venues.name: Tofanellis Gold Country Bistro
venues.blurb: Breakfast / lunch / dinner — 101 omelets
venues.notes: Award-winning breakfast; dog-friendly patio; downtown staple since 1986

venues.name: The Willo Steakhouse
venues.blurb: Steakhouse dinner
venues.notes: Classic roadhouse steakhouse; strong rustic itinerary anchor

venues.name: Three Forks Bakery & Brewing Co.
venues.blurb: Wood-fired pizza, craft beer & local wine
venues.hours: Check venue; lunch and dinner service
venues.notes: Farm-to-table sourcing; hosts Wild & Scenic Coffee Talks

venues.name: Marshalls Pasties
venues.blurb: Authentic hand-rolled Cornish pasties since 1968
venues.notes: A Gold Country institution; 203 Mill St; Cornish mining heritage in every bite

venues.name: Communal Cafe
venues.blurb: Matcha drinks, espresso & artisan donuts
venues.notes: 233 Broad St; Remedy Supply Co. espresso; local baker donuts

venues.name: Lola Restaurant
venues.blurb: Farm-to-table dining at the National Exchange Hotel
venues.notes: Named for Gold Rush outlaw Lola Montez; steak tartare, oyster mushrooms

venues.name: Fable Coffee
venues.blurb: Specialty craft coffee downtown Grass Valley
venues.notes: Indie café; great pre-gallery or pre-hike stop

venues.name: BriarPatch Food Co-op
venues.blurb: Community-owned organic grocery & deli
venues.notes: Community-owned since 1972; pasties, hot deli; great picnic provisioning

venues.name: Wyld Tiger Bakery
venues.blurb: Artisan sourdough, croissants & pastries baked fresh daily
venues.hours: Wed–Sun; morning through early afternoon; check current hours
venues.notes: Beloved local bakery in downtown Nevada City; handcrafted sourdough loaves, croissants, seasonal pastries & morning baked goods; walk-in only; arrive early — popular items sell out

venues.name: Jernigans Tap House & Grill
venues.blurb: 13 craft beers on tap + food
venues.notes: Casual taproom; popular with locals after hikes

venues.name: The River Downtown
venues.blurb: Taproom + food
venues.notes: Good post-hike / post-river option

venues.name: Nevada City Winery
venues.blurb: Production winery & tasting room — oldest in the Sierra Foothills
venues.hours: Closed Wed; Thu 2–7pm; Fri–Sat 12–7pm; Sun 12–5pm; Mon–Tue 2–6pm
venues.notes: Established 1980; full production winery & barrel room in historic Miners Foundry Garage; oldest continuously operating winery in the Sierra Foothills; walk-in tastings; revitalized the Nevada County wine industry

venues.name: Sierra Vintners Wine Trail
venues.blurb: 5 downtown tasting rooms, 8 local wineries
venues.notes: All walkable from downtown GV; Lucchesi, Avanguardia, Nevada City Winery & more

venues.name: Lucchesi Vineyards Tasting Room
venues.blurb: Award-winning estate wines, intimate tasting
venues.notes: "View Forever Vineyard" — solar-powered dry-farm; Zinfandel, Cab Franc

venues.name: Avanguardia Wines
venues.blurb: Italian-inspired Sierra Foothills wines; downtown Grass Valley tasting room
venues.notes: Award-winning Barbera, Sangiovese & Italian-heritage varietals; family-owned; downtown Mill Street location; Sierra Vintners wine trail member

venues.name: Sierra Starr Vineyard
venues.blurb: Boutique Rhône & Bordeaux varietals; downtown Grass Valley tasting room
venues.notes: Small-production estate wines; Syrah, Grenache, Cabernet Sauvignon; Sierra Vintners wine trail member; walkable downtown Grass Valley

venues.name: Smith Vineyard
venues.blurb: Fourth-generation family estate Zinfandel & Cabernet; downtown tasting room
venues.notes: Fourth-generation family winery; estate-grown grapes from Grass Valley foothills; classic Zinfandel and Cabernet; Sierra Vintners trail member

venues.name: Bent Metal Winery
venues.blurb: Artisan wines with a rock & roll spirit; downtown Grass Valley tasting room
venues.notes: Small-batch handcrafted wines; irreverent labels; named for a guitar technique; casual and fun downtown Grass Valley stop; Sierra Vintners trail

venues.name: Szabo Vineyards
venues.blurb: Hungarian-heritage Zinfandels & blends; Nevada City tasting room
venues.notes: Family-owned; Old Vine Zinfandel from Sierra Foothills hillsides; unique Hungarian heritage varietals; walkable from downtown Nevada City

venues.name: Carrington's Fine Wines
venues.blurb: Curated Sierra Foothills wine shop with tastings
venues.notes: Nevada City wine shop specializing in local Sierra Foothills producers; great single-stop way to discover the full Nevada County wine scene

venues.name: Naggiar Vineyards & Winery
venues.blurb: Premier 80-acre estate with vineyard-view patio & full tasting room
venues.notes: Premier Nevada County estate winery in Penn Valley; Italian & French varietals; panoramic vineyard views from the terrace; Sangiovese, Barbera, Vermentino; 18095 Spring Hill Rd

venues.name: Mountain Ranch Winery
venues.blurb: Small-production estate wines in the Sierra foothills
venues.notes: Intimate family estate winery; Barbera, Sangiovese & Rhône varietals grown on-site; scenic rural Nevada County setting; personalized tasting experience

venues.name: Bent Metal Winery Estate
venues.blurb: Estate vineyard & production facility; rock & roll wines made in the foothills
venues.notes: Production home of Bent Metal wines; estate-grown grapes in the Sierra foothills; vineyard setting; best to visit downtown Grass Valley tasting room walk-in or call ahead for estate visit

venues.name: Solune Winegrowers
venues.blurb: Artisan natural wines from Sierra Foothills estate & sourced fruit
venues.notes: Small-batch natural and minimal-intervention winemaking; estate and sourced Sierra Foothills fruit; distinctive and unique style; passionate producers

venues.name: Coufos Cellars
venues.blurb: Boutique family winery with Greek heritage varietals & classic Sierra wines
venues.notes: Family-owned boutique winery; unique Greek-heritage varietals alongside classic Sierra Foothills wines; intimate and personal tasting experience

venues.name: Montoliva Vineyard & Winery
venues.blurb: Tuscan-inspired estate with olive groves, wine & estate olive oil
venues.hours: Sat–Sun; check current hours; weekdays by appointment
venues.notes: Stunning Tuscan-style estate in Penn Valley; Sangiovese, Barbera & Super-Tuscan blends alongside estate olive oil; vineyard-view tasting; exceptional hospitality; 16990 Oak Hill Rd

venues.name: Gray Pine Vineyard
venues.blurb: Estate winery in the Sierra foothills named for the iconic local pines
venues.notes: Small family estate; named for the distinctive gray pine trees native to Sierra foothills; estate-grown varietals suited to the unique elevations and terroir of Nevada County

venues.name: Grass Valley Brewing Co.
venues.blurb: Brewery / taproom
venues.notes: Downtown brewery; rotating craft selection

venues.name: BrewBilt BrewHaus
venues.blurb: Beer + food gathering place
venues.notes: Family-friendly, dog-friendly brewpub

venues.name: South Yuba River State Park
venues.blurb: Covered bridge visits, river access, hiking
venues.notes: Bridgeport Covered Bridge — longest single-span wooden bridge in US

venues.name: Malakoff Diggins State Historic Park
venues.blurb: Diggins, Rim, and Humbug trails
venues.notes: CA largest hydraulic mine; dramatic cliffs; gold panning in Humbug Creek

venues.name: Buttermilk Bend Trail
venues.blurb: Easy wildflower trail above the South Yuba River
venues.notes: 1.2-mile easy trail; 100+ wildflower species; docent walks Sat-Sun 10am through Mothers Day

venues.name: Cascade Canal Trail
venues.blurb: 4.5-mile forested trail with Nevada City overlook
venues.notes: Douglas firs & dogwoods; bird's-eye view of downtown; easy grade

venues.name: Scotts Flat Lake
venues.blurb: Swimming, kayaking, fishing & camping
venues.hours: Seasonal; check Nevada Irrigation District schedule
venues.notes: 4 miles from downtown Nevada City; paddleboarding, hiking, wildlife spotting

venues.name: Deer Creek Tribute Trail
venues.blurb: Paved accessible trail through downtown riparian corridor
venues.notes: Paved, accessible; connects downtown Nevada City to nature; birdwatching

venues.name: Crystal Hermitage Garden
venues.blurb: Anandas stunning tulip & rose gardens open to public
venues.hours: Spring: daily dawn–dusk approx. March–May; call ahead
venues.notes: Photographer-led wildflower walks in spring; surreal beauty; admission by donation

venues.name: Inner Path Nevada City
venues.blurb: Yoga, meditation, spiritual shop, workshops
venues.hours: Open 7 days 10 AM–6 PM; classes vary
venues.notes: Donation yoga and frequent uplifting events; community wellness anchor

venues.name: Ananda Meditation Retreats
venues.blurb: Meditation / yoga retreats
venues.notes: Good for destination retreat packaging; Crystal Hermitage on grounds

venues.name: Mountain Stream Meditation
venues.blurb: Insight meditation community
venues.notes: Meditation-centered programming; quiet retreat option

venues.name: Rising Gaia
venues.blurb: Sound healing / Reiki / Yoga Nidra
venues.notes: Works for wellness-themed itineraries

venues.name: National Exchange Hotel
venues.blurb: Historic luxury hotel — 1856
venues.notes: Iconic 1856 property; recently renovated to boutique luxury; home of Lola Restaurant

venues.name: Holbrooke Hotel
venues.blurb: Historic downtown hotel — 1851
venues.notes: Hosted Mark Twain & US Presidents; Golden Gate Saloon; rustic-refined landmark

venues.name: Gold Miners Inn
venues.blurb: Full-service hotel in the heart of downtown Grass Valley
venues.hours: Check-in 3 PM / Check-out 11 AM; front desk 24 hrs
venues.notes: Ascend Hotel Collection; 121 Bank St — walking distance to Mill St restaurants, galleries, Center for the Arts & Sierra Vintners wine trail; heated indoor pool, hot tub & fitness center; free parking; meeting rooms

venues.name: The Parsonage 1865
venues.blurb: Historic inn / retreat venue
venues.notes: Can also host retreats and events; quiet & refined

venues.name: Sierra Mountain Inn
venues.blurb: Boutique inn steps from Center for the Arts
venues.notes: Blocks from theaters, restaurants, and shops; ask about CFA event discount

venues.name: Inn Town Campground
venues.blurb: Camping, RV, glamping
venues.notes: 2 miles from downtown; tents, RV, glamping; hosts ghost/cemetery tours

venues.name: Inn Town Glamping
venues.blurb: Deluxe canvas glamping tents
venues.notes: Canvas tents with electricity and real beds; award-winning

venues.name: Scotts Flat Lake Campgrounds
venues.blurb: Lakeshore & Cascade Shores campgrounds with RV hookups, boat ramp, swimming
venues.hours: Mid-April through mid-October; reservations at scottsflatlake.net
venues.notes: Run by Nevada Irrigation District; 850-acre lake; 5 mi east of Nevada City on Hwy 20; tent, RV (electric/water hookups), boat-in sites; general store, marina, sandy swim beach

venues.name: Rollins Lake — Long Ravine
venues.blurb: Lakeside RV park & campground with marina, store & swim beach
venues.notes: Most popular Rollins Lake campground; full hookup RV sites, tent sites, cabins; marina with boat & jet-ski rentals; store, restaurant; closest Rollins access from I-80

venues.name: Rollins Lake — Greenhorn Campground
venues.blurb: Quieter forest campground on the north shore of Rollins Lake
venues.hours: May through September; reservations at rollinslakeresort.com
venues.notes: Peaceful forested sites; tent & RV; boat ramp; swim beach; less developed than Long Ravine; family-friendly; good for fishing

venues.name: Nevada County Fairgrounds RV Park
venues.blurb: Year-round RV park at the historic fairgrounds with full hookups
venues.notes: 11228 McCourtney Rd; full hookup RV sites; walking distance to onsite Lions Lake; close to downtown Grass Valley; fills up fast during County Fair, Bluegrass Festival & Draft Horse Classic

venues.name: White Cloud Campground
venues.blurb: Tahoe National Forest campground in pine & cedar forest
venues.hours: Memorial Day through Labor Day; first-come or reserve at recreation.gov
venues.notes: On Hwy 20, ~12 mi east of Nevada City; 46 sites; tent & RV (no hookups); flush toilets; near Pioneer Trail System for hiking, biking & cross-country skiing; quiet, forested

venues.name: Skillman Family Campground
venues.blurb: Tahoe National Forest campground on Hwy 20 east of Nevada City
venues.hours: Memorial Day through Labor Day; recreation.gov for reservations
venues.notes: On Hwy 20, ~14 mi east of Nevada City; tent & small RV (no hookups); horse camp adjacent; Pioneer Trail accessible; family-friendly forest setting; pit toilets & potable water

venues.name: Englebright Lake — Boat-In Campsites
venues.blurb: Unique boat-in only campgrounds along the lake shoreline
venues.hours: Year-round; first-come; access via boat from launch ramp
venues.notes: ~100 boat-in sites scattered along 24-mi shoreline; vault toilets; no road access; bring everything; quiet, secluded; popular with paddlers & boaters; check Army Corps for site conditions

venues.name: South Yuba Campground
venues.blurb: Primitive walk-in campground at South Yuba River State Park
venues.hours: May through October; first-come-first-served; check parks.ca.gov
venues.notes: At Bridgeport — small primitive campground near covered bridge; walk-in tent sites only; pit toilets, no water; close to swimming holes, hiking & gold panning; reservations not accepted

venues.name: Broad Street Inn
venues.blurb: 1870 Victorian B&B with 6 rooms + carriage house
venues.hours: Check-in 3 PM / Check-out 11 AM; reservations required
venues.notes: 517 W. Broad St — 6 rooms plus carriage house in restored 1870 Victorian; peaceful garden setting; walking distance to downtown dining; full breakfast included

venues.name: Flume's End Inn
venues.blurb: 1860s Victorian B&B overlooking Gold Run Creek
venues.notes: 317 S. Pine St — 5 rooms with private baths & balconies overlooking the creek and gardens; one of the most romantic stays in Nevada City; full breakfast

venues.name: Piety Hill Cottages
venues.blurb: Quiet cottages on park-like grounds south of downtown
venues.notes: 523 Sacramento St — spacious rooms across an acre of park-like gardens; quiet retreat 5 min walk to downtown; pet-friendly options

venues.name: Harmony Ridge Lodge
venues.blurb: Rustic luxury B&B on 3.5 wooded acres
venues.notes: 18883 Hwy 20 — guest suites in serene forest setting; continental breakfast; 3.5 acres of private woods; close to Pioneer Trail System & White Cloud Campground

venues.name: Northern Queen Inn
venues.blurb: Pet-friendly cabins, chalets & suites with waterfall
venues.notes: 400 Railroad Ave — cabins with kitchenettes, chalets with full kitchens, suites; pet-friendly; outdoor wedding venue with waterfall backdrop; on-site Trolley Junction restaurant

venues.name: 1856 Speakeasy Suites
venues.blurb: Nine remodeled suites in a historic 1856 downtown building
venues.notes: 233 Broad St — 9 rooms remodeled in 2023 with new beds, bathrooms, furniture; available individually or as group rental; right on Broad St in downtown

venues.name: A Victorian Rose B&B
venues.blurb: Charming Victorian bed & breakfast in Grass Valley
venues.notes: 120 Winchester St — classic Victorian B&B in residential Grass Valley; walking distance to downtown

venues.name: Grass Valley Courtyard Suites & Spa
venues.blurb: Luxury boutique inn with spa & event space
venues.hours: Check-in 3 PM / Check-out 11 AM; spa by appointment
venues.notes: 210 N Auburn St — 42 rooms in restored historic Binkelman Brewery; on-site spa; 4000 sq ft event space; centrally located near downtown Grass Valley

venues.name: Outside Inn
venues.blurb: Themed motel close to downtown
venues.notes: Two blocks from downtown; quirky themed rooms; great value

venues.name: Gold Panning — Humbug Creek / Washington
venues.blurb: Try your hand at panning historic Gold Rush waterways
venues.notes: Humbug Creek at Malakoff Diggins; Washington town has public panning areas

venues.name: Ghost & Cemetery Tours
venues.blurb: Haunted tours of Gold Rush-era Nevada City
venues.hours: Seasonal; Halloween most active; Inn Town Campground hosts
venues.notes: One of CAs most haunted towns; old mines, hotels & cemeteries featured

venues.name: ASiF – Artists' Studio in the Foothills
venues.blurb: Community art center with 20+ resident artists & workshops
venues.notes: 4,000 sq ft studio; drawing, painting, ceramics, jewelry, metalsmithing, fiber arts, mixed media; all ages

venues.name: Wolf Craft School & Collective
venues.blurb: Traditional craft workshops — ceramics, fiber, shoemaking & more
venues.notes: Located at The Tinnery in downtown Nevada City; hand-built ceramics, stained glass, silversmithing, hat-making, natural dyeing; future Wolf Road campus with lodging planned

venues.name: Craftfolk Collective
venues.blurb: Community craft school in historic downtown Colfax
venues.hours: Workshops vary; free Community Craft Club Thursdays 4–6 PM
venues.notes: 38 N. Main St, Colfax; fiber arts, painting, mixed media, herbal, seasonal projects; beginners welcome; free weekly craft night

venues.name: Downtown Colfax & Heritage Museum
venues.blurb: Railroad history, historic Main Street & gold rush architecture
venues.hours: Museum: check hours; downtown always walkable
venues.notes: Former western terminus of Central Pacific Railroad; Colfax Theatre (1939), 1867 general store, free Heritage Museum in restored train depot

venues.name: Stevens Trail
venues.blurb: Historic 3.5-mile hike to North Fork American River
venues.notes: Originally used during Gold Rush; breathtaking canyon views; descends to the North Fork American River; spring wildflower showcase

venues.name: Rollins Lake
venues.blurb: Boating, swimming, fishing & camping near Colfax
venues.hours: Seasonal; check Rollins Lake Recreation Area
venues.notes: 900-acre reservoir with 26 miles of shoreline; kayaking, paddleboarding, waterfront camping; multiple campgrounds

venues.name: Snapdragon Provisions
venues.blurb: Plant-based café & local market in downtown Colfax
venues.notes: Locally owned; plant-based and gluten-free offerings; neighboring Craftfolk Collective for a craft + café combo

venues.name: Washington Hotel & Gold Panning
venues.blurb: Tiny Gold Rush town on the South Yuba River
venues.hours: Year-round; hotel bar open most evenings
venues.notes: 137-person town; hotel bar frequented by Wyatt Earp & Grover Cleveland; public gold panning at Quartz Picnic Area

venues.name: Rough and Ready
venues.blurb: Californias only town to briefly secede from the Union (1850)
venues.notes: Founded 1849; near Bridgeport Covered Bridge; wildflowers, fall colors, living history days

venues.name: Gray Pine Winery — Penn Valley
venues.blurb: Bordeaux varietals in a scenic wine cave tasting room
venues.notes: Sierra Vintners Trail member; ~350 cases/year; wine cave tours; Cab Sauv, Merlot, Petit Verdot; Western Gateway Park nearby

venues.name: Del Oro Theatre
venues.blurb: Retro single-screen cinema on Mill Street, Grass Valley
venues.notes: Classic 1942 movie palace on Mill St; a Gold Country landmark; retro marquee and single screen charm

venues.name: Sierra Cinemas Nevada City
venues.blurb: Movie theater in downtown Nevada City
venues.notes: Community cinema in Nevada City; Nutcracker screenings at Christmas; central movie destination for the county

venues.name: The Onyx Theatre
venues.blurb: 21+ indie cinema — twin 29-seat screening rooms with beer & wine
venues.hours: Daily noon–9 PM; check showtimes at theonyxtheatre.com
venues.notes: 107 Argall Way; indie, foreign & documentary focus; organic popcorn, local beer & wine; intimate date-night destination

venues.name: Nevada County Fairgrounds
venues.blurb: Year-round events, RV camping & Lions Lake — Voted California Most Beautiful Fairgrounds
venues.hours: Office Mon–Fri 8–5; events vary; RV park year-round
venues.notes: 11228 McCourtney Rd; hosts County Fair (Aug), Draft Horse Classic (Sep), High Sierra Music Fest (Jul), KVMR Celtic Fest (May), Bluegrass Festival (Jun); onsite Lions Lake & RV park

venues.name: Empire Mine Trails — Hiking, Biking & Horses
venues.blurb: 14 miles of multi-use trails through 856 acres of historic gold country forest
venues.hours: Trails open year-round; no fee for trail-only use; visitor center $5
venues.notes: Union Hill Trail Area: beginner to intermediate; bikes, horses & hikers; 1.5–10 miles; trail maps at visitor center; no bikes in historic zone

venues.name: Pioneer Trail System
venues.blurb: 65+ miles of multi-use singletrack parallel to Hwy 20 — bikes, horses & hikers
venues.hours: Year-round; may have snow in winter at upper elevations
venues.notes: Lower Pioneer (beginner–intermediate): 10mi from Harmony Ridge Market; Upper Pioneer (advanced): 10mi from White Cloud Campground; also connects to Hoot Trail & Casci Ranch Loop; maintained by Gold Country Trails Council & BONC

venues.name: Hoot Trail & Parliament Trails Network
venues.blurb: World-class flow trails accessible from downtown Nevada City
venues.notes: Hoot Trail: 1.3 miles of jumps, berms & flow built by BONC; Parliament Trails network includes Talon Show (directional MTB-only); Scotts Flat Lake Trail nearby; accessible from downtown via Pioneer Trail

venues.name: South Yuba Trail — Mountain Biking
venues.blurb: 18-mile intermediate singletrack along the South Yuba River
venues.hours: Spring through fall; may be muddy in winter
venues.notes: Trailhead at South Yuba Camp off North Bloomfield Rd; 18 miles out-and-back; river swimming access; beautiful canyon scenery; intermediate+

venues.name: Bear Yuba Land Trust Trails
venues.blurb: Conservation trails network throughout Nevada County — hiking & biking
venues.notes: Wildflower Ridge Preserve, Rice Crossing, Hirschman Pond Trail, Independence Trail; maps at bylt.org; some trails wheelchair accessible; excellent for beginners and families

venues.name: Gateway Bike Park — Penn Valley
venues.blurb: Dedicated mountain bike skills park for all levels
venues.notes: Located at Western Gateway Park in Penn Valley; purpose-built pump tracks, skills area & flow trails; great for beginners; dogs welcome

venues.name: Gold Country Trails Council — Equestrian Trails
venues.blurb: Horse-friendly multi-use trails across Nevada County
venues.hours: Year-round; Pioneer Trail has equestrian staging area off Hwy 20
venues.notes: The Pioneer Trail system was developed & maintained by equestrians; staging areas at White Cloud, Skillman & equestrian overlook; Empire Mine trails also horse-friendly; yield to horses on all shared trails

venues.name: Squirrel Creek Disc Golf — Condon Park
venues.blurb: 27-hole disc golf course rated one of the best in California
venues.notes: 660 Minnie St — tight tree-lined fairways, dense manzanita rough, year-round creek, concrete tee pads, skip-friendly red clay; home course of Gold Country Disc Golf Association (GCDGA); weekly leagues

venues.name: Hanging Oaks Disc Golf — Western Gateway Park
venues.blurb: 18-hole disc golf course in scenic Penn Valley park
venues.notes: Western Gateway Park, Penn Valley; rated 4/5 by players; also has tennis, pickleball, racquetball, baseball fields & amphitheater in same park; GCDGA hosts regular tournaments here

venues.name: Diamond Arrow Disc Golf — Nevada City
venues.blurb: 9-hole disc golf course in Nevada City
venues.notes: Convenient Nevada City course for a quick round; disc golf gear available at Brighton Street Grocery (French & Brighton)

venues.name: Bocce Ball & Outdoor Courts — Condon Park
venues.blurb: Bocce, basketball, horseshoes & lawn games in Grass Valley
venues.notes: Condon Park, 115 Bank St — bocce courts, basketball, horseshoe pits, covered group picnic area, BBQ areas, dog park, arboretum; 80-acre park a short walk from downtown GV

venues.name: Tennis Courts — Western Gateway Park
venues.blurb: Public tennis & racquetball courts in Penn Valley
venues.notes: Western Gateway Park, Penn Valley — tennis & racquetball courts plus pickleball, disc golf, baseball, amphitheater & streamside play area all in one park

venues.name: South Yuba Club — Pickleball
venues.blurb: 11-court private pickleball facility with lessons & leagues
venues.hours: Check schedule at southyubaclub.com; open play, leagues & clinics
venues.notes: Nevada County premier pickleball facility; Coach John with 30+ years experience; beginner clinics, open play, socials & leagues; membership required

venues.name: Grass Valley Pickleball Courts — Race Street
venues.blurb: Public outdoor pickleball courts with shaded areas
venues.notes: Behind the aquatic complex on Race Street; multiple courts; well-maintained; bring your own paddles; great casual drop-in play

venues.name: Western Gateway Park Pickleball — Penn Valley
venues.blurb: Pickleball, tennis & racquetball in one park
venues.notes: Western Gateway Park; pickleball + tennis + racquetball + disc golf + baseball all together; ideal for group outings

venues.name: Memorial Park — Pickleball
venues.blurb: 6 dedicated lighted courts — free, open dawn to 10 PM daily
venues.hours: Dawn to 10 PM daily; lights until 10 PM; free
venues.notes: 350 Race St, Grass Valley — 6 permanent outdoor courts, concrete, wheelchair accessible, restrooms, water fountain; Grass Valley Pickleball Club reserved times Tue/Thu/Sat 9-11am, Wed 4-6pm, Sun 12-2pm; all skill levels welcome

venues.name: Memorial Park — Tennis & Baseball
venues.blurb: Public tennis courts, baseball field, pool & playground in Grass Valley
venues.hours: Dawn to dusk (pool seasonal); free courts
venues.notes: 415 Central Ave, Grass Valley — tennis courts, baseball field, basketball, swimming pool (summer), playground, picnic areas, large grassy space; Veterans memorial grounds; adjacent to Race St pickleball courts

venues.name: South Yuba River Swimming Holes
venues.blurb: Emerald pools, granite rocks & sandy shores along the South Yuba
venues.hours: Best late June–Sept; check river conditions at syrcl.org first
venues.notes: Key access points: Bridgeport (families & small kids), 49 Crossing, Hoyt Crossing, Purdon Crossing, Edwards Crossing, Emerald Pools (high-clearance vehicle); NEVER enter when snowmelt is running high; parking fills fast — take Gold Country Stage bus #7 from Govt Center

venues.name: H2O Adventures — Whitewater Rafting
venues.blurb: Guided rafting on the South Yuba & North Fork American Rivers
venues.hours: Spring through fall; check h2oadventures.com for schedule
venues.notes: Expert guides; half-day, full-day & multi-day trips; best spring runoff season; family-friendly sections available; world-class expert kayak runs on North Fork

venues.name: Englebright Lake — Boating & Fishing
venues.blurb: Boat-access-only lake with full marina, camping & fishing
venues.hours: Year-round; Skippers Cove marina has boat rentals & launch ramp
venues.notes: Unique boat-access-only camping; rainbow & brown trout, bass, kokanee; kayak & pedal boat rentals at Skippers Cove; 815-acre lake in a steep canyon

venues.name: Fishing — South Yuba, Rollins & Scotts Flat
venues.blurb: Year-round angling for trout, bass & kokanee in rivers and lakes
venues.hours: Year-round; license required; Mountain Recreation in GV for gear & local tips
venues.notes: Rollins Lake: bass, trout & trolling; Scotts Flat: kayak & SUP rentals Memorial Day–mid-Aug (Mountain Recreation kiosk); South Yuba: fly fishing; Englebright: boat-only; Fishing Derby at Fairgrounds annually

venues.name: SYRCL Salmon Expedition
venues.blurb: Guided float trip to watch salmon spawning on the Yuba River
venues.hours: Annual — fall salmon run (Oct–Dec); check syrcl.org for dates
venues.notes: South Yuba River Citizens League guided expeditions; learn the lifecycle & ecology of Yuba salmon; also land-based hiking expeditions; an unforgettable conservation experience

venues.name: Gold Crush Climbing Gym
venues.blurb: Indoor rock climbing gym in Grass Valley
venues.notes: Top-rope, lead & bouldering; great rainy-day or river-full alternative; beginner classes available

venues.name: South Yuba Club
venues.blurb: Premier health club: gym, pool, indoor & outdoor courts
venues.notes: 130 W Berryhill Dr, Grass Valley — full health club: weights, cardio, pool, group classes, tennis, pickleball, basketball; (530) 272-7676; call about visitor day passes

venues.name: Ironworks Gym
venues.blurb: Strength & conditioning gym in downtown Grass Valley
venues.notes: 153 S Auburn St, Grass Valley — free weights, machines, cardio; (530) 272-9462; call about visitor day passes

venues.name: Generations Health Club & Spa
venues.blurb: Health club with on-site spa services in Alta Sierra
venues.notes: 22824 Industrial Pl, Grass Valley — gym, group classes, on-site spa & wellness offerings; (530) 268-2860; call about visitor day passes

venues.name: Anytime Fitness — Grass Valley
venues.blurb: 24/7 access gym chain location
venues.notes: 562 Sutton Way, Grass Valley — cardio, strength, functional training; (530) 652-4680; call about visitor day passes

venues.name: Training Zone — Grass Valley
venues.blurb: 24-hour gym with circuit training & group classes
venues.hours: Open 24 hours; staffed Mon–Fri 5:30 AM–8 PM, Sat 8 AM–5 PM
venues.notes: 722 Freeman Lane Suite B, Grass Valley — strength, cardio, group fitness; (530) 273-9663; call about visitor day passes

venues.name: Clubhouse Fitness
venues.blurb: Neighborhood gym in Alta Sierra
venues.hours: Mon–Fri 5:30 AM–9 PM; Sat–Sun 7 AM–6 PM
venues.notes: 11882 Tammy Way, Grass Valley — strength, cardio, classes; (530) 446-3507; call about visitor day passes

venues.name: Grass Valley CrossFit
venues.blurb: CrossFit affiliate — coached group strength & conditioning
venues.notes: 139 Joerschke Dr, Grass Valley — coached CrossFit classes for all levels; free intro class; (530) 263-8495; call about visitor drop-in fees

venues.name: CrossFit Gold Rush
venues.blurb: CrossFit affiliate with 40+ weekly classes
venues.hours: Mon–Fri 6 AM–7 PM, Sat 8 AM–12 PM; closed Sun
venues.notes: 1020 Whispering Pines Ln Suite E, Grass Valley — 5000 sq ft, 40+ classes/week, CrossFit + mobility + weight training; established 2012; (530) 802-0071; call about visitor drop-in fees

venues.name: Sugarloaf Mountain Hike
venues.blurb: Sunset hike with panoramic views of Nevada City & the foothills
venues.notes: Short hike from downtown Nevada City; one of the best sunset spots in the county; city-owned open space; Bear Yuba Land Trust partnership; views of downtown and surrounding ridges

venues.name: Grass Valley Farmers Market
venues.blurb: Four-decade tradition — fresh produce, live music & local vendors
venues.hours: Saturdays 8–12:30 PM & Tuesdays 8:30 AM–1 PM; Apr–Nov
venues.notes: Downtown Grass Valley; 40+ years running; free-range eggs, berries, seasonal produce, local honey & artisan goods; live music most Saturdays

venues.name: Nevada City Summer Nights
venues.blurb: Wednesday evening street festival in historic downtown
venues.hours: Wednesdays in July, 5–9 PM; road closures downtown
venues.notes: Historic section closes to cars; live music, food, drink, art vendors, bouncy house for kids; one of the best free summer evening events in the county

venues.name: Downtown Shopping — Nevada City & Grass Valley
venues.blurb: Boutiques, galleries, bookstores & artisan shops in two historic downtowns
venues.hours: Most shops open daily; hours vary
venues.notes: Nevada City: KitKitDizzi (231 Broad St — clothing, home & botanicals), Hardy Books; Grass Valley: Goodtimes (146 Mill St), local jewelers, craft studios; both downtowns walkable and charming

venues.name: Sierra Storytelling Festival
venues.blurb: "Queen of storytelling festivals" — world-class tellers in Nevada City
venues.notes: Founded by poet Steve Sanfield; 30+ years; world-class storytellers; a rare chance to unplug and experience the power of oral tradition; all ages; multiple venues

venues.name: Victorian Christmas — Nevada City
venues.blurb: Lamp-lit streets, roasted chestnuts & 19th-century magic in December
venues.hours: Two Wed evenings + three Sun afternoons in December; check nevadacitychamber.com
venues.notes: Nevada City transforms into a Victorian wonderland; street vendors in period costume, chestnuts, carolers, holiday treasures; one of the most unique Christmas traditions in California

venues.name: Cornish Christmas — Grass Valley
venues.blurb: Ye olde Cornish holiday tradition on Mill & Main Streets
venues.hours: Late Nov through 3rd Fri in December, every Friday evening; downtowngrassvalley.com
venues.notes: Grass Valley celebrates its Cornish mining heritage; carolers, mistletoe, vendors in Cornish attire, street food; starts last Friday of November; beloved annual tradition

venues.name: Penn Valley Rodeo
venues.blurb: Family-friendly full-weekend rodeo in the Sierra foothills
venues.notes: Full weekend of rodeo events; get your cowboy boots ready; one of the authentic small-town Western experiences in the foothills

venues.name: Bridgeport Gold Panning with Docents
venues.blurb: Hands-on gold panning lessons at the South Yuba River
venues.notes: Docents at Bridgeport provide step-by-step panning lessons and share Gold Rush history; combine with wildflower walks (spring) and Buttermilk Bend hike; 17660 Pleasant Valley Rd, Penn Valley

venues.name: Nevada City & Grass Valley Pools
venues.blurb: Public outdoor swimming pools — perfect when the river is high
venues.hours: Summer season (Memorial Day–Labor Day); check city sites for hours
venues.notes: Nevada City Pool at Pioneer Park (7 days/week all summer); Memorial Park Pool in Grass Valley (recently renovated, near downtown & Empire Mine); Inn Town Campground also has a pool

venues.name: Revival Float & Wellness
venues.blurb: Float therapy, infrared sauna & massage in Grass Valley
venues.hours: Tue–Sun 10 AM–6 PM; book at revivalfloat.com
venues.notes: 262 S Auburn St — 3 private float suites with 1,000 lbs Epsom salt each; infrared sauna; deep tissue, Swedish, Myofascial Release, Lymphatic Drainage, Reiki, hot stones & cupping; intro 3-float package for first-timers

venues.name: Wolf Mountain Day Spa
venues.blurb: Full-service downtown day spa with massage, facials, body treatments & nails
venues.hours: Tue–Sun 10 AM–5 PM; Mon & evenings by appointment
venues.notes: 110 E Main St — luxury spa offering acupuncture, body treatments, brow & lash, facials, hair, massage, nail services, spa packages, waxing; (530) 477-2340

venues.name: Vela Massage Retreat
venues.blurb: Massage retreat with sauna, facials & foot spa lounge
venues.hours: Mon–Wed 9:30 AM–7 PM; Thu–Sat 9:30 AM–9 PM; Sun 12:30–7 PM
venues.notes: 763B S Auburn St — Swedish, Deep Tissue, Trigger Point, Prenatal massage; facials, body scrubs, waxing, brow services; steam & infrared sauna; foot spa lounge; holistic health coaching; (530) 559-7172

venues.name: Bodyworks Salon & Day Spa
venues.blurb: Spa packages, massage & salon services in Grass Valley
venues.notes: Established Grass Valley day spa offering massage, facial, hair & spa-package services; book online at salonbodyworks.com

venues.name: Bel Capelli Salon-Spa
venues.blurb: Boutique salon-spa in downtown Nevada City
venues.notes: Combined salon and spa offering massage, facials, hair, and body services in historic downtown Nevada City

venues.name: Golden Bee Holistic Center
venues.blurb: Reflexology, Reiki, hypnotherapy & holistic healing in Nevada City
venues.hours: By appointment Mon–Fri 9:30 AM–6 PM; weekend appointments available
venues.notes: 310 1/2 Broad St — reflexology, Reiki, spiritual integral counseling, life coaching, hypnotherapy, medical hypnosis; on-site gift store with locally-sourced healing artwork; (530) 264-7383

venues.name: Wild Mountain Yoga Center
venues.blurb: Premier yoga studio — 2 locations, 90+ weekly classes
venues.hours: Daily; check wildmtyoga.com for class schedule
venues.notes: 121 S Church St, Grass Valley (above Yuba Blue) & Nevada City location; Hatha, Vinyasa, Yin, Restorative, meditation; international teacher trainings; community donations welcome; drop-in friendly

venues.name: Warmth Studios
venues.blurb: Hot yoga & Pilates — Bikram, Vinyasa, Yin & Inferno Hot Pilates
venues.notes: 155 Spring Hill Dr Ste 206, Grass Valley — classes at 98–103 degrees; locker rooms, mat & towel rentals; Bikram 26+2, hot Vinyasa, Yin, Yoga Nidra, Inferno Hot Pilates; established 2003 as Bikram Yoga GV/NC

venues.name: The Yoga Dive / Kinfolk Yoga
venues.blurb: Community yoga studio offering classes, workshops & art therapy
venues.notes: Neighborhood studio in downtown Grass Valley; Kinfolk Yoga offers ceramics + yoga workshops, art therapy & bodywork; 20+ years of combined teaching; beginner-friendly & drop-in classes

venues.name: Golden Mandala Yoga Studio
venues.blurb: Yoga, meditation & spiritual community in Nevada City
venues.notes: Nevada City yoga and meditation community; Hatha, gentle yoga, and meditation classes; welcoming to all levels

venues.name: Ananda Expanding Light Retreat
venues.blurb: Northern California premier yoga & meditation retreat since 1968
venues.notes: Daily guided yoga postures & meditations; year-round programs; teacher training & certification; Hatha, Bhakti, Kriya yoga; also Crystal Hermitage Garden on campus

venues.name: Nevada County Arts Council
venues.blurb: Hub of the arts in Nevada County — events calendar, artist directory & cultural programs
venues.hours: Office hours vary; arts calendar updated continuously at nevadacountyarts.org
venues.notes: 100 N Church St, Grass Valley; State-Local Partner with California Arts Council; manages GVNC & Truckee Cultural Districts; publishes MUSE magazine; comprehensive Arts & Culture Calendar covering all county events; free resource for visitors

venues.name: Arts & Culture Calendar — NCAC
venues.blurb: Most comprehensive arts & culture events calendar in Nevada County
venues.notes: nevadacountyarts.org/calendar — concerts, theater, art exhibits, live performance, film screenings, poetry, dance & more across all of Nevada County; submit your own event free

venues.name: MUSE Magazine
venues.blurb: Free annual guide to art & culture in Nevada County
venues.hours: Annual publication; pick up free at chambers, venues & visitor centers
venues.notes: 10,000 print copies distributed across Grass Valley, Nevada City & Truckee; also along I-80 & Hwy 20 from Sacramento to Reno; yearly cultural map, events calendar, artist & business directories; digital edition at nevadacountyarts.org/muse

venues.name: Open Studios Nevada County
venues.blurb: Annual self-guided tour of working artist studios
venues.notes: 30th anniversary event — studios open county-wide across Nevada City, Grass Valley & surrounding areas; meet artists in their working spaces; unique art purchases direct from creators

venues.name: Mardi Gras Nevada City
venues.blurb: Colorful Fat Tuesday parade with floats, Burning Man art cars & community spirit
venues.hours: Annual — Sunday before Fat Tuesday (February); downtown Nevada City
venues.notes: Nevada City version of Mardi Gras; dozens of festive floats, marching groups, Burning Man art cars; fun & family-friendly; beads, costumes & community spirit

venues.name: Nevada City Chinese New Year Celebration
venues.blurb: Lunar New Year celebration honoring Gold Rush Chinese heritage
venues.hours: Annual — January/February (date varies with lunar calendar)
venues.notes: Honors the rich Chinese heritage of the Gold Rush era in Nevada County; Firehouse No.1 houses the Hou Wang Temple altar from Grass Valley Chinatown; community celebration with food, music & cultural traditions

venues.name: Sugar Pine Music Festival
venues.blurb: 4-day family camping music festival in the Sierra foothills
venues.notes: Multi-day camping festival; diverse music lineup; kid zone; costume themes; arts and craft vendors; food and drink; family-friendly outdoor experience

venues.name: InConcert Sierra
venues.blurb: Classical and chamber music concert series in Nevada County
venues.hours: Concert series runs fall through spring; check inconcertsierra.org
venues.notes: Professional-quality classical & chamber music performances at intimate Nevada County venues; local talent alongside visiting artists; beloved community institution

venues.name: North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center
venues.blurb: Hootenannies, concerts, poetry readings & art shows on the San Juan Ridge
venues.notes: 17894 Tyler Foote Rd, Nevada City — unique 1800s schoolhouse hosting concerts, poetry, theater, square dances & community gatherings on the historic San Juan Ridge; intimate and authentic

venues.name: CATS — Community Asian Theatre of the Sierra
venues.blurb: Multicultural theater productions in Gold Rush country
venues.notes: Promoting cultural diversity through multicultural theater, events & workshops in Western Nevada County; performs at Nevada Theatre; unique perspective on the regions diverse heritage

venues.name: Sierra Stages
venues.blurb: Professional theater company performing at the Nevada Theatre
venues.notes: High-quality live theater at the historic Nevada Theatre (1865); produces a mix of classic and contemporary plays; a cornerstone of the local performing arts scene

venues.name: Trails & Vistas
venues.blurb: Art hikes fusing visual art, music & nature in the Sierra landscape
venues.hours: Annual — Earth Day weekend (April) and summer; trailsandvistas.org
venues.notes: Unique outdoor arts experience — artists, performers & musicians integrated into nature hikes; celebrating 20+ years; artists from Lake Tahoe Basin to East Coast; tickets required; one of the most distinctive arts events in California

venues.name: Creatives Meetup — NCAC
venues.blurb: Monthly arts community networking event in Grass Valley & Nevada City
venues.hours: Monthly — check nevadacountyarts.org for dates & locations
venues.notes: Free monthly gathering for artists, makers & creatives; held alternately in Grass Valley and Nevada City; great way for visitors to connect with the local arts community; all welcome

venues.name: Nisenan Heritage Day
venues.blurb: Annual celebration of the indigenous Nisenan people of Nevada County
venues.hours: Annual — fall; check nevadacountyarts.org for dates
venues.notes: Honors the Nisenan Tribe, the original inhabitants of the Nevada City area; cultural education, art, and community; Nevada City Rancheria Nisenan Tribe recently purchased ancestral land at Yulica; connects Gold Rush history with indigenous heritage
